segments.text:
P e rtussis
CLS-N390 Vaccines: S c ience and S o ciety

Bordetella pertussis
Gram negative bacilli
V ery small
F astidious = hard to grow
H umans are only reservior

Transmission
Very contagious – aerosolizes
Spread via coughing or sneezing – or being around each other and sharing air space
Bacteria attach to cilia (tiny, hair-like extensions) lining upper respiratory tract
Hairs help expel bacteria into the air
Many babies who get pertussis are infected by siblings, parents, or caregivers who don’t know they have it
R 0 value = 15 – 17; 94% vaccination for herd immunity

Symptoms
Typically develop within 5 to 10 days of exposure
Catarrhal Stage
Early stage: 1 – 2 weeks
Runny nose
Low-grade fever
Mind, occasional cough
Highly contagious
Paroxysmal Stage
1 – 6 weeks; may be as long as 10 weeks
Fits of numerous, rapid coughs followed by “whooping:” sound
Vomiting and exhaustion after coughing fits
Convalescent Stage
2 – 3 weeks
Susceptible to other respiratory diseases
Recovery is gradual
Coughing lessens, but fits may return

Complications
1 out of 4 (25%) get pneumonia
1 out of 100 (1%) have convulsions (violent, uncontrolled shaking)
3 out of 5 (61%) have apnea (slowed or stopped breathing)
1 out of 300 (0.3%) have encephalopathy (disease of brain)
1 out of 100 (1%) die

Pertussis Vaccine Timeline
1914
1930s
1948
2005
Whole-cell vaccine licensed
Formalin inactivated whole-cell vaccine developed
Combined with diphtheria and tetanus (DTP)
Acellular vaccine licensed

Characteristics of Vaccine
Subunit vaccine – contains purified, inactivated components of B. pertussis cells
Efficacy – 80% - 85%
Immunity – approximately 5 - 10 years
Several vaccines available – different components
<12 months 4 doses; 12+ months – 3 doses;
booster @ 4-6 years; booster for family

Vaccine Options
DTaP – pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, and acellular pertussis
<7 years old
Tdap – adult diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, and acellular pertussis
>7 years old
DTaP- HepB -IPV – combination of pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, acellular pertussis, hepatitis B, poliovirus
DTaP-IPV-Hib – combination of pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, acellular pertussis, poliovirus, and Haemophilus influenzae type B
Other
